text: The robotic-assisted hysterectomy procedure involves clamping, fulgurating, and cutting the round, broad, and utero-ovarian ligaments, ensuring excellent hemostasis on both medial and lateral pedicles. As the surgeon cuts through the tissue, the ovaries are preserved and gently fall away due to the patient's preference.

The surgeon transects the lower cardinal ligaments, exposing the uterine vessels. The right ureter is identified and positioned safely away from the surgical field before proceeding with the takedown of the anterior bladder flap and fulguration of the uterine vessels.
The surgeon dissects the anterior bladder flap to access the vesicle uterine avascular space, followed by transection of the utero sacral ligaments, leading to entry into the posterior vagina.
The surgeon completes the circumferential transection of the cervix, separating it from the vaginal cuff.

The surgeon applies gentle pressure to deliver the uterus from the surgical field. The vaginal cuff is closed using figure-of-eight vicryl sutures through the mucosa, ensuring complete closure and hemostasis.
